brand.name: Ordre
brand.tagline: Purchase orders, without losing the thread.

landing.heroTitle: Know exactly what's committed, what's been invoiced, and how much is left to spend.
landing.heroSubtitle: Ordre tracks purchase orders, runs them through your team's approval rules, and reconciles every invoice line by line. No spreadsheets. No lost email threads.
landing.pillarCommitBody: Raise a purchase order with its line items — description, category, quantity, price. Save as a draft or send it up for approval.
landing.pillarApproveBody: The right people get notified, approve with a comment or reject with one. Every decision is logged, stamped, and searchable.
landing.pillarReconcileBody: Upload the invoice, map it back to the lines, and the remaining balance on each line updates instantly. When it hits zero, close the PO.
landing.previewTitle: One PO, four numbers that matter
landing.footerNote: Built for audiovisual production teams and small offices.

meta.title: Ordre — Purchase orders, approvals & invoice reconciliation
meta.description: Raise purchase orders, run them through your team's approval rules, and reconcile every invoice line by line. Monochrome. Opinionated. No spreadsheets.

landing.heroBullet1: Free forever for a single project
landing.logosLabel: Trusted by small teams in Barcelona, Berlin and beyond

contrast.beforeItem1: No single place to see what's still open.
contrast.beforeItem2: Approvals live in Slack DMs and screenshots.
contrast.beforeItem3: Invoices pile up and nobody knows what's been paid.
contrast.beforeItem4: The producer re-keys the same data three times.
contrast.afterTitle: One board. Live totals. Searchable log.
contrast.afterItem1: Every open PO in one list, with remaining budget next to it.
contrast.afterItem2: Approvals recorded on the PO itself, with name and timestamp.
contrast.afterItem3: Invoices attach back to lines — the maths do themselves.
contrast.afterItem4: Supplier details filled once and reused across every PO.

landing.howTitle: Commit, approve, reconcile — in that order.
landing.howSubtitle: The three steps that take a purchase order from idea to fully-invoiced. Ordre enforces the order and records the evidence along the way.
landing.featuresTitle: Everything a small team needs. Nothing that turns it into accounting software.
landing.featuresSubtitle: We resist feature creep. If it isn't core to running POs through approval and reconciliation, it stays out.

suppliers.title: Supplier directory
suppliers.body: Tax ID, payment terms, bank details, contract PDFs. Reusable across every PO.

categories.title: Categories & codes
categories.body: Your chart-of-accounts codes assigned per line, CSV-importable, matching your accountant's format.

roles.title: Role-based access
roles.body: Owner, editor, approver, viewer — Firestore-enforced, so the rules can't be bypassed from the client.

approvals.title: Approvals queue
approvals.body: Pending POs land in your queue. Approve with a bill, reject with a reason, everything's logged.

invoices.title: Line-by-line invoicing
invoices.body: Map invoice totals to PO lines. The remaining balance per line updates live.

reports.title: Reports & charts
reports.body: Committed-vs-invoiced over time, top suppliers, spend by category. Not a replacement for your accountant — a signal feed.

i18n.title: Catalan / Spanish / English
i18n.body: Native translations with correct date, currency and number formatting per locale.

darkMode.title: Dark mode, by default
darkMode.body: Monochrome design that respects your system preference. No brand colours, no neon, no gradient glows.

landing.quotesTitle: A few people we've built this with.

verdera.body: We were tracking ninety suppliers in a spreadsheet. Ordre killed the spreadsheet in a week — and our approval cycles dropped from three days to half a day.
verdera.name: Helena Vives

tallerpol.body: I used to chase invoices on WhatsApp. Now I just open the PO, see what's left on each line, and ask for that. Huge relief.
tallerpol.name: Pol Benavent

escenic.body: The approvals log is the piece I didn't know I needed. Every 'who-signed-off-on-this' question has a single answer now.
escenic.name: Sílvia Gomis

data.a: In Firebase (Google Cloud), europe-west1 region. You own it, you can export it any time, we don't share it with third parties. More in the Privacy Policy.

pricing.q: Is the free plan really free?
pricing.a: Yes. One project, unlimited POs, suppliers and invoices inside it, 300 MB of file storage. We'll never add a surprise paywall to something that was free.

cancel.a: Yes. Ordre Pro will be month-to-month with no lock-in when self-serve billing opens. During the beta, Pro is free for invited teams.

multi.q: Can I use Ordre for multiple companies?
multi.a: Each project is its own space with its own suppliers, POs, approvals and storage quota. Ordre Pro lifts the single-project limit.

security.a: Google Sign-In for auth, Firebase App Check (reCAPTCHA v3) on every request, Firestore/Storage security rules scoped to project membership with owner/editor/approver/viewer roles.

import.q: Can I import my existing suppliers or accounting codes?
import.a: Accounting codes import via CSV today. Supplier import is on the roadmap. Invoices — intentionally not, every invoice needs a human to map it back to the PO lines.

stats.languagesLabel: so international teams can work together
stats.freeLabel: start for free, scale when you're ready
stats.setupLabel: from sign-up to your first approved PO

showcase.title: Approve from anywhere, in seconds.
showcase.body: Pending POs land in a clean queue, sorted by who's blocking what. Approve with a comment, reject with a reason — every decision is logged with a timestamp.

landing.finalCtaTitle: Stop losing track of what you've committed.
landing.finalCtaBody: Create your first project in under a minute. Free forever for small teams.

auth.bullet1: Raise and approve POs in minutes.
auth.bullet2: Reconcile every invoice line by line.
auth.bullet3: Free for a single project, no card needed.

login.title: Welcome back.
login.subtitle: Pick up where you left off. We sign you in with your Google account.
login.error: Couldn't sign you in. Try again in a few seconds.

signup.title: Get started with Ordre.
signup.subtitle: One Google click and your workspace is ready. No credit card, no friction.
signup.terms: By continuing you agree to the Terms and Privacy policy.
signup.error: Could not create your account. Please try again.

members.title: Project members

errors.userNotFound: This person doesn't have an Ordre account yet. Ask them to sign in with Google first.
errors.alreadyMember: {{name}} is already a member of this project.
errors.targetProjectLimit: {{name}} is already an owner or editor of another project. Free plan accounts can only own or edit one project. Add them as Viewer or Approver instead, or ask them to upgrade to Pro.
errors.permissionDenied: You don't have permission to make this change.
errors.generic: Couldn't add the member. Please try again.
errors.roleChangeFailed: Couldn't update the role. Please try again.
errors.removeFailed: Couldn't remove the member. Please try again.

roleCaption.owner: Can edit everything and manage members.
roleCaption.editor: Can create and change POs, suppliers, and invoices.
roleCaption.approver: Can approve POs and record bill payments. Cannot edit POs.

members.cannotRemoveYourself: An owner can't leave while they're the only one.
members.ownerOnly: Only the owner can make changes here.

app.welcomeBody: Pick an existing project or create a new one to get started.
app.stubBody: Dashboard, PO list, reconciliation detail, PO creation, supplier directory, and the approvals queue are being ported from the reference prototype to Firestore-backed pages.

pricing.title: Easy to start, grow at your pace.
pricing.subtitle: One project to try the whole thing. Upgrade to Pro when you want more.

plans.freeSubtitle: To try Ordre with a single project.

pricing.featureFree2: Unlimited suppliers, POs and invoices per project
pricing.fallbackNote: Final price confirmed at checkout, in your currency.

consent.title: Cookies, the short version.
consent.body: We use cookies to keep you signed in. With your permission we also measure how Ordre is used (Firebase Analytics, PostHog — both hosted in the EU) so we can make it better. No ads, no third-party tracking.
consent.customiseBody: Choose which categories are on. You can change this later from the Privacy page.

necessary.name: Necessary
necessary.body: Sign-in session, theme, selected language. Ordre can't work without these.

analytics.name: Analytics & diagnostics
analytics.body: Firebase Analytics + PostHog: aggregate usage, funnels, and automatic error reports. Hosted in the EU, never shared with ad networks.

terms.metaDescription: The rules that apply when you use Ordre. Plain language. No dark patterns.
terms.title: Terms of Service

acceptance.title: Acceptance of terms
acceptance.body: By creating an account or using Ordre, you agree to these terms. If you don't agree, please don't use the service. These terms apply to everyone on your team who uses your workspace.

account.title: Your account
account.body: You need a valid email address and a Google Sign-In account to use Ordre. Keep your credentials secure — you're responsible for any activity on your account. Accurate account information (name, email) helps your teammates know who's approving what.

plans.title: Plans and billing
plans.body: Ordre Free includes a single project and 300 MB of file storage. Ordre Pro lifts those limits (10 GB per project, unlimited projects) and is invite-only during our beta — self-serve billing is coming. Prices and included quotas may change with reasonable notice.

content.title: Your content
content.body: You keep ownership of everything you upload or create in Ordre — purchase orders, invoices, supplier data, attachments. You grant us a limited license to store and process this content solely to provide the service. We do not sell or share your data with third parties beyond the infrastructure providers required to run Ordre (Firebase / Google Cloud, based in the EU where possible).

acceptableUse.title: Acceptable use
acceptableUse.body: Don't use Ordre to store illegal content, attempt to disrupt the service, scrape data you don't own, or circumvent security measures (including App Check enforcement, storage quotas, or role permissions). We reserve the right to suspend accounts that clearly violate these principles.

termination.title: Termination
termination.body: You can close your account at any time by contacting us. We can suspend or close accounts that breach these terms or that are inactive for an extended period. On termination you can export your data for a reasonable window before deletion.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.body: Ordre is provided "as is" during our beta. We take availability seriously but can't guarantee uninterrupted service. To the maximum extent allowed by law, we aren't liable for indirect or consequential damages (lost profits, lost data beyond our backups, etc.).

governing.title: Governing law
governing.body: These terms are governed by the laws of Spain. Any disputes will be resolved in the courts of Barcelona, without prejudice to mandatory consumer-protection laws that apply where you live.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Questions about these terms? Reach us at

privacy.metaDescription: What data we collect, why, and your rights under GDPR.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy

controller.title: Who we are
controller.body: Ordre is operated from Barcelona, Spain. For the purposes of GDPR we act as the data controller for account data and as a data processor for the content you upload (purchase orders, invoices, attachments).

data.title: What we collect
data.body: Account data: name, email, profile picture (all from Google Sign-In). Workspace content: projects, suppliers, POs, invoices, attachments you upload. Technical data: anonymized usage logs, error reports, App Check attestations. We don't use tracking cookies or third-party analytics beyond Firebase's own.

purpose.title: Why we use it
purpose.body: To provide the service, authenticate you, enforce storage and plan limits, send transactional emails (project invites, password resets), and investigate security incidents. We never use your content to train AI models or sell it to third parties.

sharing.title: Who we share it with
sharing.body: Our infrastructure provider is Google Cloud (Firebase). Data is stored in the europe-west1 region. We use a Namecheap Private Email transactional SMTP to send project-invite emails. We do not share data with ad networks, data brokers, or any other third party.

storage.title: How long we keep it
storage.body: Active accounts: for as long as you have an account with us. Closed accounts: we delete your content within 30 days of account closure, except where legal retention obligations apply (invoices may need to be retained under Spanish tax law; those are your copies, held in our system on your behalf).

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: You can access, export, correct, or delete your data at any time — log into Ordre or email us. You have the right to object, restrict processing, or lodge a complaint with the AEPD (Spanish Data Protection Agency) if you think we've mishandled your data.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.body: Necessary: Firebase Auth session cookies to keep you signed in, plus local-storage for your theme and language. Analytics (only with your consent): Firebase Analytics and PostHog first-party cookies to measure aggregate usage and diagnose errors. No advertising or third-party tracking cookies.

paywall.unlockProjectsBody: The Free plan covers 1 project. Upgrade to Ordre Pro to create as many as you need and invite your whole team.
paywall.betaBody: Ordre Pro is invite-only during our beta. Drop us a line and we'll set you up within a day.
paywall.dismissedToast: You're still on Free. You can upgrade any time.
paywall.disabledNotice: Web billing isn't configured in this environment.

subscription.portalUnavailable: The billing portal becomes available after your first purchase.

poActions.approveSubtitle: The decision is logged with your name, a timestamp, and the comment.

poActions.closeConfirm: Close this PO? It won't accept new invoices.

poActions.deleteConfirm: Permanently delete this PO and all its data? This cannot be undone.

poDecision.approveSubtitle: The decision is logged with your name, a timestamp, and the comment.

projectSettings.subtitle: Advanced settings for {{project}}. Only the owner can make changes here.
projectSettings.storageHint: Total size of all files uploaded to this project — invoices, PO attachments, supplier documents.
projectSettings.descriptionHint: How you'd describe it to someone outside the project.
projectSettings.ownershipHint: Pick another member as the owner. You'll be demoted to editor.
projectSettings.ownershipConfirm: Hand ownership of {{name}}? You'll be demoted to editor and can't undo this unless the new owner promotes you back.
projectSettings.archiveBody: Hide the project from the active list without deleting any data. You can restore it anytime.
projectSettings.archiveConfirm: Archive {{name}}? No data is lost and you can reactivate it anytime.
projectSettings.dangerBody: Delete this project permanently. Only allowed when the project has no suppliers, categories or purchase orders. If there's data, archive it instead.
projectSettings.deleteDisabledReason: {{suppliers}} suppliers, {{categories}} categories and {{pos}} POs block the permanent delete. Archive or empty it first.
projectSettings.deleteConfirm: This can't be undone. Type the exact project name to confirm.
projectSettings.deleteError: Couldn't delete the project. Please try again.
projectSettings.ownerOnlyBanner: Only the owner can make changes on this page.
projectSettings.archivedBanner: This project is archived. Edits still go through, but it won't show up in the main list.
